q: The angle of elevation of the top of a tower from a point on the ground, which is 30 m away from the foot of the tower, is 30°. The height of the tower is:
A ladder 15 m long just reaches the top of a vertical wall. If the ladder makes an angle of 60° with the wall, then the height of the wall is:
sol: The angle with the wall is 60°, so the angle with the ground is 30°. sin 30° = h/15 => 1/2 = h/15 => h = 7.5 m.

q: If the length of the shadow of a tower is √3 times its height, then the angle of elevation of the sun is:
The angle of depression of a car parked on the road from the top of a 150 m high tower is 30°. The distance of the car from the tower is:

A kite is flying at a height of 60 m above the ground. The string attached to the kite is temporarily tied to a point on the ground. The inclination of the string with the ground is 60°. The length of the string is:
If the altitude of the sun is 60°, the height of a vertical tower that will cast a shadow of length 20 m is:

The angle of elevation of the top of a building from the foot of a tower is 30° and the angle of elevation of the top of the tower from the foot of the building is 60°. If the tower is 50 m high, the height of the building is:

From a point on the ground, the angles of elevation of the bottom and the top of a transmission tower fixed at the top of a 20 m high building are 45° and 60° respectively. The height of the tower is:
sol: Distance to building = 20/tan 45° = 20 m. Total height = 20 * tan 60° = 20√3 m. Tower height = 20√3 - 20 = 20(√3-1) m.

q: A 1.5 m tall boy is standing at some distance from a 30 m tall building. The angle of elevation from his eyes to the top of the building increases from 30° to 60° as he walks towards the building. The distance he walked is:
The shadow of a tower standing on a level ground is found to be 40 m longer when the Sun's altitude is 30° than when it is 60°. The height of the tower is:
If the angle of elevation of a tower from a distance of 100 m from its foot is 60°, then the height of the tower is:
The line drawn from the eye of an observer to the point in the object viewed by the observer is called:
sol: This is the definition of the line of sight.

q: An observer 1.5 m tall is 28.5 m away from a chimney. The angle of elevation of the top of the chimney from her eyes is 45°. The height of the chimney is:
sol: Height above eye = 28.5 * tan 45° = 28.5 m. Total height = 28.5 + 1.5 = 30 m.

q: If the ratio of the height of a pole and its shadow is 1:1, then the angle of elevation of the sun is:
The angle of elevation of the top of a tower as seen from a distance of 50 m from its foot is 30°. The height of the tower is:

Two poles of equal heights are standing opposite each other on either side of a 80 m wide road. From a point between them on the road, the angles of elevation of the tops are 60° and 30°. The height of the poles is:

The angle of elevation of the top of a tower from two points at distances of 4 m and 9 m from the base of the tower and in the same straight line with it are complementary. The height of the tower is:
sol: h/4 = tan θ, h/9 = cot θ. h²/36 = tan θ * cot θ = 1 => h = 6 m.

q: A bridge across a river makes an angle of 45° with the river bank. If the length of the bridge across the river is 150 m, then the width of the river is:
As the observer moves towards a vertical object, the angle of elevation of its top:
sol: The closer you get, the higher you have to look, so the angle increases.

q: The angle of depression of an object on the ground from the top of a 75 m high tower is 30°. The distance of the object from the foot of the tower is:
If a pole 6 m high casts a shadow 2√3 m long on the ground, then the Sun’s elevation is:

A circus artist is climbing a 20 m long rope, which is tightly stretched and tied from the top of a vertical pole to the ground. If the angle made by the rope with the ground level is 30°, the height of the pole is:

A tree breaks due to storm and the broken part bends so that the top of the tree touches the ground making an angle of 30° with it. The distance between the foot of the tree to the point where the top touches the ground is 8 m. The height of the tree is:
The angle of elevation of the top of a tower from a point 15 m away from its foot is 60°. The height of the tower is:
A balloon is connected to a meteorological station by a cable of length 200 m inclined at 60°. Find the height of the balloon from the ground (assume cable is straight):
The angle of elevation of a cloud from a point h meters above a lake is θ. The angle of depression of its reflection in the lake is φ. The height of the cloud is:
o: h(tan φ + tan θ) / (tan φ - tan θ)
h(tan φ - tan θ) / (tan φ + tan θ)
sol: This is a standard derivation where height H = h(tanφ + tanθ) / (tanφ - tanθ).

q: A vertical pole 10 m long casts a shadow 10 m long. The angle of elevation of the sun is:
If the angle of elevation of a tower is doubled, the height of the tower:
sol: tan(2θ) is not 2*tan(θ), so the height increases but is not doubled.

q: The angle of depression of the top and bottom of a 8 m tall building from the top of a multi-storeyed building are 30° and 45°. The height of the multi-storeyed building is:
In a right triangle ABC, if ∠B = 90° and ∠A = 30°, then the ratio of BC to AB is:
sol: tan A = BC/AB => tan 30° = 1/√3. Ratio is 1:√3.

q: If the height of a tower and the distance of the point of observation from its foot are both increased by 10%, the angle of elevation of its top:
sol: tan θ = h/d. New tan θ' = (1.1h)/(1.1d) = h/d. So angle remains same.

The angle of elevation of the sun when the shadow of a pole h meters high is h/√3 meters long is:
When the line of sight is above the horizontal level, the angle formed is:
sol: Looking up creates an angle of elevation.

q: When the line of sight is below the horizontal level, the angle formed is:
sol: Looking down creates an angle of depression.

The angle of elevation of the top of a tower from a point on the ground 30 m from the foot of the tower is 30°. The height of the tower is:
If the angle of elevation of the sun is 45°, then the length of the shadow of a tower is:
From the top of a 60 m high building, the angles of depression of the top and the bottom of a tower are 45° and 60° respectively. The height of the tower is:
sol: Distance d = 60/tan 60° = 20√3. Height difference = d * tan 45° = 20√3. Tower height = 60 - 20√3 = 20(3-√3) m.

q: The top of a ladder 10 m long reaches a window 5 m high. The angle of inclination of the ladder with the horizontal is:
A pole casts a shadow of length 2√3 m when the sun's elevation is 60°. The height of the pole is:

sol: As the sun gets higher, the shadow gets shorter.

q: A 10 m high pole stands vertically on the ground. The angle of elevation of its top from a point 10√3 m away from its foot is:
An observer 1.2 m tall is 20 m away from a tower 21.2 m high. The angle of elevation of the top of the tower from the eye of the observer is:
sol: Height above eye = 21.2 - 1.2 = 20 m. tan θ = 20/20 = 1 => θ = 45°.

The angle of elevation of the top of a tower from two points at distances a and b from the base and in the same straight line with it are complementary. The height of the tower is:
sol: h/a = tan θ, h/b = cot θ. h²/ab = 1 => h = √(ab).

q: From the top of a 75 m high lighthouse, the angles of depression of two ships are 30° and 45°. If one ship is exactly behind the other on the same side, the distance between the ships is:

A 1.2 m tall girl spots a balloon moving with the wind in a horizontal line at a height of 88.2 m from the ground. The angle of elevation changes from 60° to 30°. The distance travelled by the balloon is:

The angle of elevation of the top of a hill from the foot of a tower is 60° and the angle of elevation of the top of the tower from the foot of the hill is 30°. If the tower is 50 m high, the height of the hill is:

A straight highway leads to the foot of a tower. A man standing at the top of the tower observes a car at an angle of depression of 30°. The car is approaching the foot of the tower with a uniform speed. Six seconds later, the angle of depression of the car is found to be 60°. The time taken by the car to reach the foot of the tower from this point is:
sol: Let dist be d. h/d = tan 30, h/(d-x) = tan 60. d = h√3, d-x = h/√3. x = d - d/3 = 2d/3. x in 6s. Remaining d/3 in 3s.

The angle of elevation of the top of a tower from a distance of 50 m is 45°. The height of the tower is:
If the angle of elevation of a cloud from a point h m above a lake is θ and the angle of depression of its reflection is φ, then tan θ = (H-h)/d and tan φ = (H+h)/d. Solving for H gives:
o: H = h(tan φ + tan θ) / (tan φ - tan θ)
H = h(tan φ - tan θ) / (tan φ + tan θ)
H = h tan θ / tan φ
H = h tan φ / tan θ

sol: The side opposite the angle θ in a height and distance problem is the height.
